pi_items.source: Verified by reading setup_task.sh, which downloads the file and saves it to this exact path.
The setup script explicitly downloads from raw.githubusercontent.com/opengeos/data/main/raster/landsat.tif. Inspecting the TIFF tags confirms it is LANDSAT_LE7_TOA_5YEAR imagery.
Inspecting the downloaded GeoTIFF using rasterio confirms it has exactly 4 bands.

Statistical analysis of the TIFF bands reveals characteristic Rayleigh scattering (Band 1 min > Band 2 > Band 3), high inter-band correlation among the first three bands, and typical Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) values between Band 4 and Band 3. This physically proves the file contains standard Landsat 7 visible and NIR bands, directly contradicting the SWIR1/NIR/Red/Green claim.
The task description explicitly requires the agent to assign the Red channel to band_2, the Green channel to band_3, and the Blue channel to band_4, matching this metadata claim exactly.

privileged_info_summary: The task involves opening a real Landsat 7 TOA GeoTIFF image downloaded from the opengeos GitHub repository in SNAP Desktop. The image contains exactly 4 bands. Although the task instructions and metadata claim the bands correspond to SWIR1, NIR, Red, and Green, pixel statistical analysis (Rayleigh scattering, inter-band correlation, and NDVI calculation) proves the bands are actually Blue (Band 1), Green (Band 2), Red (Band 3), and Near-Infrared (Band 4). Regardless of this factual mislabeling in the prompt, the agent is explicitly instructed to configure the false-color RGB image by assigning the Red channel to band_2, the Green channel to band_3, and the Blue channel to band_4.
